news.headline: JCP&L is upgrading power system in Mercer County, benefiting nearly 1,000 customers. Byline: Holmdel, N.J., Feb. 21, 2025.
news.summary: Around 1,000 customers are set to gain from the initiative in HOLMDEL, N.J., as of February 21, 2025. Jersey Central Power & Light (JCP&L), a branch of FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE), is enhancing its power grid in sections of Mercer County to reduce the occurrence and severity of disruptions.

news.headline: PMI refutes Stanley Black & Decker's lawsuit over "Stanley" brand ownership in food/beverage containers.
news.summary: PMI is the owner of the "Stanley" brand for the products it is currently marketing, holding the trademark for both "Stanley" and the winged bear logo in the food and beverage container sector. A lawsuit filed by Stanley Black & Decker aims to leverage PMI's accomplishments with the Stanley brand.

news.headline: US stock futures fall; inflation data awaited. CPI forecasted at 2.9% YoY, 0.3% MoM. 10-year Treasury yield at 4.54%. Dow down 0.17%, Nasdaq down 0.07%. SPY at $604.45, QQQ at $527.67. Market reacted mixed to tariff announcements and Powell's testimony.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday as Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell testified before Congress. Economists predict a 2.9% year-over-year consumer price index with a 0.3% monthly increase in January. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.54%, and the two-year yield at 4.29%. There is a 95.5% chance of no interest rate changes in the March Fed meeting. Benchmark indices were down except for the Russell 2000. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust fell by 0.14% to $604.45, while the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF dropped 0.06% to $527.67. In the last session, Tesla shares dropped, DoorDash surged, Marriott tumbled, and Coca-Cola rose. Powell stated no urgency to shift interest rates and refrained from discussing trade policy. Communication services, health care, and consumer discretionary sectors fell, while others rose. The Dow closed at 44,593.65, up 0.28%.

news.headline: President Trump shifts stance on inflation, now concedes prices may take longer to reduce after promising to lower them immediately.
news.summary: During his campaign, President Trump promised to reduce costs from the beginning. However, his administration has admitted that achieving this goal will require more time than initially anticipated.

news.headline: Discover the booming export industry in Guangzhou, producing cheap goods flooding global markets tariff-free, along the Pearl River.
news.summary: In Southern China, a rapidly expanding industry along the Pearl River is producing affordable clothing and toys, which are being exported worldwide without duties.

news.headline: Inflation surges past expectations in January: CPI rises to 3% year-over-year, monthly growth at 0.5%. Core inflation increases to 3.3%.
news.summary: Price pressures rose more than anticipated in the first month of the year, driven by increases in energy goods and service items, impacting investor outlook on potential Federal Reserve interest rate adjustments. The headline Consumer Price Index climbed to 3% year-over-year, exceeding median economist predictions of 2.9%. Monthly inflation also surpassed expectations, increasing by 0.5% compared to the anticipated 0.3%. This rise in inflation is the most significant since August 2023. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, rose from 3.2% to 3.3% year-over-year, with a monthly increase of 0.4%.

news.headline: Euro tariffs on American whiskey set to snap back on March 31 unless extended, highlighting impact of trade war.
news.summary: As the world approaches another trade dispute, European tariffs on American whiskey are set to increase after March 31 unless an extension is approved. This means that the liquor industry may face challenges as tariffs snap back into place, impacting the trade relationship between Europe and the US. This decision could have significant consequences for the American whiskey market in terms of sales and revenue.

news.headline: G-20 displays unity amid global order turmoil sparked by Trump's actions.
Company anticipates a loss in the first half due to impairment charges. Details to be revealed. #BusinessNews
President Trump intensifies tariff threats on global scale, potentially disrupting global trade with actions surpassing those in his first term.
news.summary: In just under a month as president, the current administration has already implemented trade measures that have the potential to significantly disrupt the global trading system. These recent actions far surpass in magnitude the trade policies enacted throughout the entirety of the president's first term in office.

news.headline: Inflation data surpass expectations: CPI up 3%, fuel oil rose 6.2%, Fed rate cut expectations pushed to 2025 and 2026.
news.summary: The January inflation report showed a higher-than-expected increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI), with a 3.0% year-over-year rise, exceeding the prior reading and expectations of 2.9%. This marked the fourth consecutive annual inflation increase, with a significant monthly increase of 0.5%, the largest since August 2023. Fuel oil had the biggest jump at 6.2% month-over-month, while the index for meats, poultry, fish, and eggs rose 1.9%, including a 15.2% increase for eggs. Following this report, traders adjusted their expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, pushing the first anticipated cut to December 2025 and the second to no earlier than September 2026. This shift is also reflected in the betting odds on the CFTC-regulated Kalshi platform, where a single rate cut in 2025 is seen as the most likely scenario.

news.headline: Verizon will redeem debt securities on March 18, 2025, as announced on Feb. 14, 2025.
news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, NASDAQ: VZ) disclosed plans to redeem certain notes on March 18, 2025. The redemption date was confirmed on February 14, 2025. Verizon did not specify the exact value or quantity of the notes being redeemed in the announcement.

news.headline: THEON's FY 2024 preliminary results exceed guidance, signaling a prosperous year with expectations of continued growth in FY 2025.
UnitedHealth is under DOJ investigation for medical billing practices, according to Bloomberg.
Trump criticizes Europe's VAT system, saying it gives unfair trade advantages. Here's how it began.
news.summary: The president has expressed concerns about the Value-Added Tax (VAT) system utilized in Europe, arguing that it creates a trade disparity benefiting other nations. The VAT system was initially implemented to help bolster revenues in the 1960s, evolving into a key source of income for various European countries. However, the president believes this system now poses challenges for U.S. businesses, with VAT rates averaging around 21% across Europe. This issue has sparked discussions about potential reforms to address the perceived trade advantages enjoyed by other countries.

news.headline: Global Policy Advisors predict Federal Reserve involvement in proposed U.S. sovereign wealth fund.
news.summary: Salar Ghahramani, a Global Policy Advisor, has predicted that the Federal Reserve will play a significant role in a planned sovereign wealth fund for the United States.

news.headline: January's CPI rose to 3% YoY, highest since August 2023, driven by food and energy prices. Experts warn of inflation impact on future interest rates.
news.summary: Inflation data for January surprised experts by rising to 3% year-over-year, surpassing expectations. Monthly inflation saw its highest increase since August 2023, attributed to surges in food and energy prices. Additionally, core inflation, excluding these factors, also rose, indicating broader concerns. Senior investment strategist Charlie Ripley from Allianz Investment Management noted that potential price hikes due to President Donald Trump's tariffs haven't been factored in yet. The market may need to reassess its outlook on interest rates in light of these developments. For more information, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Elon Musk visits Dept. of Labor, could impact inflation trends significantly.
news.summary: In the latest data release, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 5.5%, marking a significant decrease from the previous month. The economy also added 250,000 new jobs, exceeding expectations and indicating a positive trend in the job market. These numbers reflect a steady recovery from the economic downturn and offer hope for continued growth in the coming months. Additionally, the report highlighted a 2.3% increase in average hourly wages, providing further evidence of improving conditions for workers. Overall, the data paints a promising picture of recovery and resilience in the economy.

news.headline: Abbott declares 405th consecutive quarterly dividend - 59 cents per share, paid since 1924.
news.summary: Abbott, based in Abbott Park, Illinois, announced on February 21, 2025, that its board of directors approved a quarterly common dividend of 59 cents per share. This dividend payment, set to be distributed on May 15, 2025, will be the company's 405th consecutive quarterly dividend since 1924. Abbott, listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ABT, has shown a strong commitment to returning value to its shareholders through consistent dividend payouts for over a century.

news.headline: DOGE gains traction in Washington as Elon Musk leads efforts to revamp government and cut spending. Wall Street not impressed.
news.summary: After only a brief period since the beginning of the DOGE initiative, Elon Musk has made numerous bold statements regarding the project. He is spearheading an effort to bring significant changes to the federal government, reduce supervision of private businesses, and reduce expenditures.

news.headline: Stocks rebound after hot inflation data, S&P 500 down 0.2%, Nasdaq flat, CPI rises 3%, core inflation climbs to 3.3%. Market uncertainty due to inflation data.
news.summary: The inflation report released on Wednesday showed higher-than-expected numbers, with the headline Consumer Price Index (CPI) rising 3% year over year in January, surpassing economist forecasts of 2.9%. Monthly inflation increased 0.5%, higher than December's 0.4% gain. Core inflation, excluding food and energy, rose to 3.3% year over year and 0.4% monthly. This led to initial market rattling, with the S&P 500 down 0.2% and the Nasdaq 100 flat, before recovering later in the day. Small-cap stocks fared worse, with the Russell 2000 slipping 0.8%. Investors regained confidence to buy the dip, mitigating losses. Traders responded by scaling back expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, now only projecting one reduction by December.

news.headline: JAS acquires IAA, a Netherlands-based logistics provider, on Feb 21, 2025, expanding its services globally.
news.summary: Atlanta-based company JAS, specializing in logistics and supply chain solutions, has recently finalized its acquisition of International Airfreight Associates B.V. (IAA), a Netherlands-based provider of Air, Ocean, and Road Freight services. This strategic move further solidifies JAS's position as a global leader in the industry. The acquisition enhances JAS's capabilities in providing comprehensive shipping solutions to its clients, expanding its reach in key transportation sectors. This acquisition represents a significant investment for JAS and is expected to result in increased operational efficiency and growth opportunities for the company.

news.headline: eXoZymes' CEO Michael Heltzen & team to ring Nasdaq bell to celebrate IPO at $8/share on Nov. 14, 2024.
news.summary: Monrovia, California - On February 19, 2025, eXoZymes, Inc. (Nasdaq: EXOZ) unveiled plans for CEO Michael Heltzen and team to participate in ringing the Nasdaq Stock Market closing bell. The event, scheduled for Wednesday, February 19, 2025, celebrates the company's achievement following an initial public offering of common stock at $8 per share on November 14, 2024. eXoZymes specializes in AI-engineered enzymes that convert sustainable feedstock into crucial chemicals, medicines, and biofuels, marking them as pioneers in this field.

news.headline: US government explores potential trade deal with China to increase American goods purchases and investments.
news.summary: American officials are currently exploring the possibility of reaching an agreement with China to increase its buying of U.S. goods and investments within the country.

news.headline: Trump 2.0 seeing quick executive actions, major cuts to staffing, funds; impact on economy imminent.
news.summary: The rapid pace of new executive actions, coupled with substantial reductions in staffing and federal funding at key agencies, is projected to directly affect economic output.

news.headline: GivBux, Inc. eliminates $100,000 in convertible debt, boosting financial stability without diluting shareholder value.
news.summary: In Newport Beach, California, on February 21, 2025, GivBux, Inc. (OTC: GBUX), a publicly traded Super App and charitable giving platform, disclosed the retirement of $100,000 in outstanding convertible notes. This decision aims to decrease the company's liabilities without the issuance of more stock, safeguarding shareholder value.

news.headline: Former Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer to face Senate HELP Committee for confirmation hearing today.
news.summary: Today, former Representative Lori Chavez-DeRemer will go before the Senate HELP Committee for her confirmation hearing.

news.headline: In January, US egg prices hit record-high of $4.95; prices increased by 15.2% contributing to 0.5% rise in food costs.
news.summary: In January, the inflation rate exceeded expectations as grocery prices surged, driven by a significant rise in egg prices. The average cost of a dozen eggs skyrocketed to a record-breaking $4.95, marking a notable 15.2% increase. This spike contributed to a 0.5% overall rise in home food expenses. Charlie Billelo, Creative Planning's chief market strategist, emphasized that the 238% surge in egg prices over the past four years is the largest on record. This data reinforces the significant impact of rising egg prices, leading to concerns about escalating food costs.

news.headline: Dow Jones plummeted by 450 points due to hot CPI report; Nvidia partner Super Micro soared on earnings.
news.summary: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones plummeted by 450 points following a strong CPI report, just before Fed Chair Powell's upcoming testimony. Meanwhile, Super Micro Computer Inc., a partner of Nvidia, experienced a significant surge in their earnings.

news.headline: Dow Jones futures drop post Trump tariff news, S&P 500 near record high. Coinbase, Twilio, Roku report earnings.
news.summary: The S&P 500 index is close to its all-time high as the implementation of new tariffs by President Trump has been delayed. Investors are advised to pay attention to the earnings reports of companies like Coinbase, Twilio, and Roku. Additionally, Dow Jones futures have fallen following the news of Trump's tariffs, causing the market to approach its peak levels. Investors are encouraged to reevaluate their strategies in light of these developments.

news.headline: Powell tells Congress Fed not ready to cut rates, as inflation at 3%, above forecasts. US debt path 'unsustainable'.
news.summary: Chair Jerome Powell of the Federal Reserve reaffirmed the central bank's unwavering stance on monetary policy during testimony before the House Financial Services Committee. Powell highlighted that although inflation is easing, it has not yet reached a level that would warrant interest rate cuts. Following his appearance before the Senate Banking Committee, Powell emphasized the necessity for further advancements to attain the Fed's 2% inflation goal. Pointing to the latest Consumer Price Index report, he noted that annual inflation stood at 3%, slightly surpassing the projected 2.9%. Core inflation, excluding volatile food and energy prices, also exceeded expectations, reaching 3.3% and suggesting sustained price pressures. Powell acknowledged the high CPI reading compared to forecasts and called for patience, emphasizing the importance of not overreacting to a single data point.

news.headline: Fabrinet's Q2 earnings exceed expectations with revenue at $833.6 million and adjusted EPS at $2.61. Datacom demand moderates.
news.summary: Fabrinet (NYSE:FN) saw its shares trading lower in premarket on Tuesday after revealing its second-quarter results on Monday. The company generated revenue of $833.6 million, surpassing the consensus estimate of $814.3 million. Additionally, its adjusted EPS stood at $2.61, beating the consensus of $2.50. Seamus Grady, Chief Executive Officer of Fabrinet, highlighted the strong performance in the telecom sector, driven by the growing demand for datacenter interconnect products and the initial success from recent systems wins. While there has been a slight moderation in datacom demand in the short term, Grady remains optimistic about the company's overall performance.

news.headline: Coffee prices surge over 100%, hitting historic highs. Americans, with 66% daily consumers, face inflated costs. Arabica coffee trades at $3.90 per pound, a 100% increase from a year ago. Supply chain disruptions may lead to $10 coffee cups.
news.summary: The U.S. has the largest coffee market with 66% of the population consuming an average of 2-3 cups daily. However, coffee prices have hit record highs, increasing by 100% from a year ago to $3.90 per pound. Coffee is outperforming major stock market indices and even Bitcoin in 2025, with Arabica coffee futures rising for 10 straight sessions. This surge could lead to a $10 cup of coffee and impact the entire supply chain, causing concerns about rising inflation. Companies in the coffee industry may benefit from these surging prices.

news.headline: Dow Jones drops on weak retail sales; GameStop stock surges with bitcoin news. ~95 characters
news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average experienced a decline following the release of disappointing retail sales data. In contrast, GameStop's stock saw a significant increase as news of discussions about bitcoin circulated in the stock market. This sharp rise in GameStop's stock price occurred on the same day when the Dow Jones registered a drop due to the underwhelming retail sales figures. The specific numbers for the Dow Jones decline and the GameStop surge were not provided in the original news snippet.

news.headline: US retail sales fell by 0.9% in January, higher than expected, which raises concerns over the economy's growth.
news.summary: In January, consumer spending experienced a significant decline, as retail sales plummeted by 0.9%, a stark contrast to the expected 0.2% decrease. This data from the Commerce Department, released recently, has sparked concerns about the vitality of economic growth. The figures also indicate a potential obstacle for the Federal Reserve as it considers its next policy decisions. December had seen a 0.7% increase in retail sales, pointing to a robust end to the holiday shopping season before the decline started. Excluding auto sales, there was still a 0.4% drop, failing to meet earlier predictions. These statistics raise uncertainties about the economic outlook going forward.

news.headline: Dow Jones closes near day's low, Nasdaq near highs. GameStop and Samsara showed performance. Live updates.
news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones index lagged behind other major indexes in the stock market. GameStop saw an increase in stock value, while Samsara experienced a breakout from its base. The Dow Jones closed near its daily low, contrasting with the Nasdaq's close near its daily high.

news.headline: S&P 500 nears all-time high, up 1.5% on the week, driven by strong corporate earnings despite concerns over tariffs.
news.summary: Stocks are experiencing mixed performance around midday on Friday, with major indices hovering close to the flatline as investors process disappointing retail sales figures, potential tariffs, and geopolitical updates. The S&P 500 has seen a 1.5% increase this week and is on the verge of reaching a new record high. This rise can be attributed to robust corporate earnings, with S&P 500 companies reporting a 16.4% year-over-year growth in fourth-quarter earnings, according to FactSet. Additionally, 146 S&P 500 firms have expressed concerns about tariffs in recent earnings calls, marking the highest number since mid-2019. Despite worries about widespread tariff implementations, stocks are holding steady as traders navigate through these uncertainties. For more details, please visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: S&P 500 hits high despite Meta and Amazon down; Dow Jones Futures fall. Axon Enterprise breaks out.
news.summary: The S&P 500 reached a record high driven by growth, despite declines in Meta Platforms, Amazon, and other major companies. Axon Enterprise experienced a breakout. Dow Jones futures dropped following the S&P 500's high. This news was highlighted in an article on Investor's Business Daily.

news.headline: Anglo American partners with Codelco in $5 billion deal to develop Los Bronces and Andina sites, producing 2.7 million tons of copper by 2030. Anglo American shares surge 5%.
news.summary: Mining company Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) has recently entered into a $5 billion agreement with Chilean state-owned miner Codelco to collaborate on the development of their neighboring Los Bronces and Andina operations. This partnership aims to solidify Anglo American's presence in the primary copper-producing region. Operations at these sites are scheduled to commence in 2030 and are projected to generate an extra 2.7 million tons of copper over a span of 21 years. In 2023, Anglo American's Los Bronces mine produced 215,000 tons of copper, while Codelco's Andina division, encompassing the Rio Blanco and Sur Sur mines, produced 164,500 tons. The agreement states that both companies will maintain complete possession of their assets and continue operating independently. In response to the news, Anglo American's shares surged by more than 5%. CEO Duncan emphasized the importance of copper in the company's strategic growth plans.

news.headline: Huntington Ingalls Q4 earnings miss with $3B revenue and $3.15 EPS; Operating cash flow drops to $393M in 2024.
news.summary: Huntington Ingalls Industries Inc (NYSE:HII) saw a drop in shares following the release of its fourth-quarter FY24 results. Sales and service revenue rose by 5.4% year-over-year to $3.00 billion, falling short of the expected $3.05 billion. Ingalls Shipbuilding generated $736 million (-8.0% Y/Y), Newport News Shipbuilding brought in $1.59 billion (-4.6% Y/Y), and Mission Technologies earned $713 million (-4.3% Y/Y). Operating income decreased by 64.7% Y/Y to $110 million, with a margin contraction of 616 bps to 3.7%. Segment operating income was $103 million (-68.8% Y/Y) with a margin of 3.4%, down 696 bps Y/Y. The EPS was reported at $3.15, down 54.3% Y/Y, missing the projected $3.31. Operating cash flow dropped to $393 million in 2024, and free cash flow to $40 million, contrasting with $970 million and $692 million respectively.

news.headline: New in VEXI Villages: Gala Games reveals Hearton, a exclusive character with just 200 units, adding to gameplay.
news.summary: Gala Games introduces Hearton, an exclusive character in VEXI Villages, showcasing a distinct heart-shaped appearance. Limited to just 200 units, Hearton aims to elevate the gaming adventure for players through its unique design.

news.headline: In January, U.S. inflation spikes as Consumer Price Index rises by 3% y-o-y; Wall Street rallies on Ukraine peace talks and delayed tariffs.
news.summary: The U.S. economy is currently facing inflation concerns, but despite this, Wall Street remains strong as investors focus on positive developments such as peace talks in Ukraine and a tariff plan by President Donald Trump that seems to be less severe than anticipated, at least for now. In January, the headline Consumer Price Index increased by 3% year-over-year, surpassing expectations of 2.9%. Monthly inflation also saw a significant rise of 0.5%, exceeding forecasts of 0.3% and marking the largest increase since August 2023. The surge in energy prices, particularly fuel oil by 6.2% and a record-breaking 15% increase in egg prices, were significant contributors to this inflation. In addition, there were noticeable inflationary pressures within the services sector. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, also experienced an increase. For further details, the complete article can be accessed via Benzinga.com.

news.headline: AI-powered Signs by NVIDIA boosts ASL learning, bridging communication gaps among deaf and hearing communities.
news.summary: NVIDIA has joined forces to create Signs, an AI-powered platform aimed at enhancing the learning and growth of American Sign Language. This collaboration intends to close the communication divide between the deaf and hearing populations.

news.headline: DOJ investigates UnitedHealth for Medicare billing fraud, accused of inflating diagnoses. Already under scrutiny for drug pricing.
news.summary: The Justice Department has initiated a civil fraud investigation into UnitedHealth Group Inc’s Medicare billing practices. The investigation, launched in recent months, will focus on UnitedHealth’s Medicare Advantage plans and physician groups amid reports that the company received billions in federal payments for questionable diagnoses. In addition to this probe, UnitedHealth is facing scrutiny for significant price markups on specialty generic drugs by its pharmacy benefit management unit, along with CVS Health Corp.’s CVS Caremark and Cigna Corp.’s Express Scripts, as detailed in a report by the Federal Trade Commission in January. The Wall Street Journal reported that Medicare made substantial payments to UnitedHealth based on added diagnoses.

news.headline: Dow Jones falls before Fed minutes release, Arista Networks AI stock plunges on earnings. Dow falls and Arista stocks dive.
news.summary: On Wednesday, the Dow Jones experienced a decline as investors awaited the release of the Federal Reserve minutes scheduled for later that day. Arista Networks, an artificial intelligence company, saw a substantial drop in its stock value following a disappointing earnings report. The market index fell by a certain number of points, reflecting a cautious sentiment among traders as they anticipated further insights from the Fed. This news was reported by a well-known financial publication.

news.headline: Sweden makes monthly contributions in Swedish kronor to the EU budget, exchanged into euros by a central bank within the EU.
news.summary: Membership in the European Union requires Sweden to make monthly financial contributions to the EU budget. The Swedish government pays these contributions in Swedish kronor, which are then exchanged into euros by a central bank in the EU.

news.headline: Sompo Holdings splits into two global segments on April 1, 2025: Sompo P&C and Sompo Wellbeing, aiming to offer better services.
news.summary: On February 14, 2025, Sompo Holdings Inc. revealed its restructuring plan to divide its business into two key segments starting from April 1, 2025. The integrated financial group aims to focus on delivering health, wellbeing, and financial protection. The newly formed segments are Sompo Property and Casualty (re)insurance (Sompo P&C) and Sompo Wellbeing. This strategic move is designed to create a more efficient and organized setup, ensuring resilience, innovation, and sustainability across the organization.

news.headline: Walgreens reduces Cencora stake, transferring 6.1M shares and $20M cash payment, selling 1.3M shares, reaping $300M.
news.summary: Walgreens Boots Alliance, Inc. (NASDAQ:WBA) recently finalized an agreement for the early settlement of specified prepaid variable share forward transactions tied to Cencora, Inc. (NYSE:COR). In this arrangement, Walgreens Boots will be transferring roughly 6.1 million Cencora shares to financial institutions and issuing a net cash payment amounting to around $20 million. Additionally, the company completed the sale of the remaining 1.3 million shares of Cencora pledged in the early settled contracts, resulting in a sum of approximately $300 million from the early settlement and the simultaneous share sale. Post-settlement, WBA will still have 12.6 million Cencora shares under the remaining prepaid contracts, set to mature ... Further details are provided on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Dow Jones falls after inflation data; Block, Rivian stocks plummet on earnings. Significant market movement today.
news.summary: On Friday, the Dow Jones experienced a decline following the release of crucial inflation figures. Both Block and Rivian shares plummeted significantly on the stock market today due to their earnings reports. This was a significant drop in the market, highlighting the impact of the inflation data.

news.headline: Genians achieves 20 years of profitability, with $6.8 million profit in 2024, reflecting 52.2% increase. #cybersecurity #profitable
news.summary: A company based in San Jose, California, Genians, known for its Universal ZTNA solutions, celebrates a significant milestone of 20 straight years of profitability, showcasing its robust financial standing and leadership in the cybersecurity sector. Serving a vast clientele of over 5,000 customers globally, Genians is setting benchmarks in network security and spearheading Zero Trust adoption. Notably, in 2024, the company attained a record operating profit of around $6.8 million (KRW 98 billion), showcasing a remarkable 52.2% growth.
